features.name: Make the cache something I can see
features.desc: You have a fetch cache, a full route cache, a router cache. Three things deciding whether my code even runs, and all of them invisible. A cache you can't see isn't a feature, it's a bug with a delay on it. Put a panel in dev that shows what's cached and why. That alone kills half your GitHub issues.

features.name: One rendering story, in one sentence
features.desc: SSR, SSG, ISR, RSC, use client, use server. Every one is a real idea and together they're a fog. The framework isn't too complex — the explanation is. Lead with one mental model a junior gets on the first read. The power can stay. The vocabulary has to go.

features.name: next dev tells me the one slow thing
features.desc: Builds get slow and nobody knows why. Don't give me a flame graph. Give me one line: this route, this dependency, this much. Name the single biggest cost and I'll fix it today. The rest is noise.

killed: the Pages Router (you have two — pick one)
three ways to load a font
a plugin API for the plugin API
config for things that should have one right default

verdict.quote: Let's be honest about what happened here. You built the best way to ship React, and then you kept building more ways to ship React. Two routers. Five caches. A server component, a client component, and a footgun on every line between them. The power is real — I'm not arguing that. The problem is nobody can hold it in their head anymore, and a tool you can't hold in your head stops being a tool. So pick one router. Make the cache something I can actually see. Say the rendering story in one sentence a kid understands on the first read. You don't need more surface. You need the nerve to delete half of it. Do that and you're still the React framework. Don't, and something simpler eats you in three years.
